title: Lasierpa hispidula

The Annonaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as custard-apple or soursop family. The classification of Lasierpa hispidula is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. This plant is native to tropical regions of Asia.

side_effects: Lasierpa hispidula, also known as the poison dart plant, contains a toxic compound called kalihinol A, which can cause various side effects if ingested.
